title: Creating a Web Output Filter Plug-in
breadcrumbs: Creating a Web Output Filter Plug-in
text: Creating a Web Output Filter Plug-in Uniface provides a generic mechanism that enables you to add your own JavaScript or HTML to web pages generated from dynamic and static server pages. Create a component that has an operation called filterOutput , with an IN parameter for the server page instance name, and an INOUT parameter for the content to be injected into the server page. For example: operation filterOutput params string pWebComponentInstanceName : In string pResponseStream : InOut endparams <Your code here> end ; operation filterOutput Copy The UGOMEZ component contains useful functionality, for example, for reading parameters from an INI file, which you may wish to use as-is or adapt. Note: The UGOMEZ component provides a caching mechanism that can be useful if you want to make your own component reusable. To make use of this, the component must be detached. You are entirely free to implement the component as you see fit. Edit the application assignment file to add the component name to the UWEBOUTPUTFILTERS logical. For example: [LOGICALS] UWEBOUTPUTFILTERS = myplugin Copy The components defined by this logical are activated by the Uniface USYSHTTP component in the order specified by the logical, after it has generated the response to the browser request. Related Links USYSHTTP The USYSHTTP component is a global web application request processor that handles all web requests sent to a Uniface Server via the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD).
